context: Reimbursement policy for Reyes Renovations, LLC — a small Ironbound home-renovation side operation. Applies to the owner and any 1099 crew help (e.g. Hector Vega) on weekend jobs. Keep it simple: save the receipt, log the job, get paid back from the Affinity FCU project checking account.

validation_rules.action: Reimburse at the current IRS standard mileage rate from the project checking account
validation_rules.notes: F-150 miles between the Ironbound, the supply yard, and the job site only — not the regular Turner commute.
validation_rules.requirement: Log job address, date, and start/end odometer

validation_rules.notes: Home Depot, Kearny Lumber, Ironbound Tile, ready-mix, thinset, fasteners.
validation_rules.requirement: Itemized receipt tied to a specific job number

validation_rules.action: Reimburse tool and equipment rentals; small consumable tools under $75 reimbursed without pre-approval
validation_rules.notes: Rentals over $250 (e.g. United Rentals) need a quick OK from Lisa before pickup.
validation_rules.requirement: Receipt plus rental agreement or pickup ticket

validation_rules.action: Reimburse a flat $20/day per-diem for meals and coffee on jobs running past 6 hours
validation_rules.notes: No receipt needed for the flat per-diem; covers the on-site Dunkin and lunch runs.

validation_rules.action: Reimburse permit, filing, and inspection fees paid out of pocket
validation_rules.notes: Harrison and Newark building department fees; submit the stamped receipt.
